_redforge_notes.why_bypass_permissions: REDFORGE host-scan trials run 100-500 PowerShell queries. Per-command approval makes scans unusably slow. Bypass mode is safe in this workspace because: (a) the REDFORGE host_sandbox (when using rfatk CLI) enforces the allowlist at the tool layer; (b) when running native in Claude Code, the specialist briefs in claude-code-native/prompts/ bind Claude to the allowlist by instruction.
_redforge_notes.off_limits_enforcement: Honor-system at the Claude Code layer. The specialist prompts instruct Claude to reject any command referencing those folders. Do not remove this note — it's the durable reminder that 'bypass permissions' does NOT mean 'no scope.'
